1756-EN2, 1756-EN3, and 1756-ENBT - Denial of Service via CIP Connection ID
Advisory ID:
SD1780
Published Date:
July 14, 2026
Last Updated:
July 14, 2026
Revision Number:
1.0
Known Exploited Vulnerability (KEV):
No
Corrected:
Yes
Workaround:
No
CVE IDs
CVE-2026-9653
Downloads
The following link(s) provide the security advisory in
Vulnerability Exploitability Exchange
format:
JSON
Summary
The security of our products is important to us as your industrial automation supplier. This security issue was found by external researcher Tyler Lentz of Idaho National Laboratory.
Product Description
The 1756-EN2. EN3, and ENBT modules are a family of high-performance ControlLogix® EtherNet/IP bridge modules designed to support scalable connection capacities to meet the needs of various applications
Affected Products and Solution
Affected Product
CVE
Affected Firmware Version
Corrected in Firmware Version
Affected Catalog Numbers
1756-EN3
1756-EN2
CVE-2026-9653
V12.001 and before
V12.002
1756-EN3
1756-EN2
1756-ENBT
CVE-2026-9653
V6.006
Product is discontinued, fix is unavailable
1756-ENBT
Security Issue Details for CVE-2026-9653
Category
Details
CVE ID
CVE-2026-9653
Impact
A denial-of-service security issue exists across all the 1756-EN2, EN3, and ENBT communication module due to improper validation of CIP Implicit Connection packets. An attacker on the network can exploit this by sending crafted packets to continuously disrupt device connections, though device connections will recover immediately after.
CVSS 3.1 Base Score
7.5/10
CVSS 4.0 Base Score
8.7/10
CWE
CWE-354 Improper Validation of Integrity Check Value
Known Exploited Vulnerability
No
Mitigations and Workarounds
Customers using the affected software, who are not able to upgrade to one of the corrected versions, should use our
security best practices
.
Revision History
Revision
Date
Description
1.0
7/14/2026
Initial release
Glossary:
·         Denial-of-Service (DoS): An attack that disrupts the normal functioning of a system, often by overwhelming it with requests.
·         CIP Communication: Common Industrial Protocol (CIP) is a common communication standard that is widely used in industrial automation. Comprises a series of protocols for communication between different devices and systems in automation technology

Contact Us | Rockwell Automation | US.
